title: salix cordata var. platyphylla
intro: Salix cordata var. platyphylla, also known as riverbank willow, is a variety of willow tree native to North America, characterized by its broad, rounded leaves. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in its bark, which contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin and used to treat pain, reduce inflammation, and alleviate fever. The health benefits of Salix cordata var. platyphylla include its ability to soothe digestive issues, relieve menstrual cramps, and lower blood pressure, making it a popular herb in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used internally or applied topically to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
